reference: Serotonin or five-hydroxytryptophan might be the cause of the increase in your liver enzymes. It is metabolized in the liver, and this could cause an elevation of your liver enzymes. You need to do the following: one. Taper off your dose of five-hydroxytryptophan, lowering five milligrams every week until you completely stop the drug. two. Keep your weight within normal range. three. Wait for three weeks after stopping five-hydroxytryptophan and then retest your liver enzymes. If your liver enzymes are still elevated, you need to go to a gastroenterologist who will conduct a full panel of tests for hepatitis C.
text: Serotonin, or 5-hydroxytriptophan, might be the cause of the increase in your liver enzymes. It is metabolized in the liver, and this could cause an elevation of your liver enzymes. You need to do the following: (1) taper off your dose of 5-hydroxytriptophan, lowering 5 mg every week until you completely stop the drug; (2) keep your weight within normal range; (3) wait for 3 weeks after stopping 5-hydroxytriptophan and then re-test your liver enzymes. If your liver enzymes are still elevated, you need to go to a gastroenterologist who will conduct a full panel of tests for hepatitis C.
